tasks.label: Go out to the club with the friend group

memory.summary: The group is pushing me to go out, but my midterm tomorrow makes skipping those final review hours feel like a risky gamble I can't afford right now.
My study grind paid off, but I missed the club window; Mina's last call feels like a hollow reminder now that the deadline has passed.
The extra hour finally pushed my prep past 0.89, silencing the nagging doubt that I'm gambling with tomorrow's grade.
The grind hit 0.98, silencing my doubt about gambling on the midterm. I feel a hollow ache for the missed club window now that the deadline is gone.
The 1.5-hour grind secured the grade, but the hollow ache for the missed club window lingers as I collapse into exhaustion.
The extra hour silenced my doubt, but the hollow ache for the missed club window now feels heavier than the relief of finishing.
The work is done, but the hollow ache for the missed club window feels heavier than the relief of finishing.

desires: Not have to explain to Mina and Jae why I missed the club window again, even though I know they won't actually get mad.
Feel that hollow ache for the lost social time fade away so my brain stops nagging me about it while I try to relax.
Just get this laundry done quickly without having to plan anything or worry about what happens next week.
Prove to myself that grinding until 2 AM was worth it by not feeling like a total failure when I look at the empty calendar.

goals.description: Lock in the final 2% of review notes for tomorrow's midterm immediately; I can't risk looking unprepared to prof_assignment.
goals.rationale: Academic outcomes (0.5 weight) are my only metric that matters right now. The doubt about skipping review hours is a threat to my grade, so I must eliminate it before anything else.

goals.description: Silently scroll through memes for 10 minutes as a dopamine hit instead of doing laundry or reaching out to Mina; the deadline is Sunday and the midterm is the only real pressure point.
goals.rationale: Risk tolerance is high and procrastination bias is strong. Laundry and social obligations feel like friction I should avoid. Memes provide immediate stress relief without the effort of actually connecting with the friend group or cleaning up.

agent.last_reasoning: Midterm is done (100%); no academic gain left. Social friction of club attendance outweighs low-priority closeness gains. Sleep fixes stress/energy debt so I can avoid tomorrow's grind without burning out later.
